VALUE: module_canonical_status and assertModulesCanonical suggest repository-wide cross-module protection, but the registered canonical Switching script run_switching_canonical.m never consults the registry; assertModulesCanonical is optional and appears at only one analysis callsite with a caller-chosen module list; execution_status and writeSwitchingExecutionStatus do not record that a module gate ran, so SUCCESS is indistinguishable from ungated runs.

Every execution path that can perform cross-module analysis must declare the full set of participating modules and invoke assertModulesCanonical (or an equivalent single choke-point check) before any cross-module data or path access; that gate must be reflected in authoritative run artifacts; and coverage must include the canonical Switching entrypoint if the policy is claimed for canonical Switching—or the policy scope must explicitly exclude that entry and any ungated scripts.
